description_long: This object is a retro-style computer monitor with a bulky, angular housing and a prominent, slightly curved green display screen. The casing is primarily a matte gray color, with a wide, flat base that extends forward, possibly serving as a support or integrated keyboard area. The monitor's screen is deeply recessed within the housing, providing a hooded effect that reduces glare and adds to the vintage aesthetic. The sides and top of the monitor are smooth and unadorned, with no visible vents, buttons, or ports. The overall design is utilitarian, with sharp edges and a robust, industrial feel. There are no visible logos, decorative elements, or external controls on the monitor.
description: A bulky, retro computer monitor with a green screen, matte gray casing, and a wide, flat base. The design is angular and industrial, with no visible buttons or logos.
description_view_0: Front view showing the green display screen recessed within a gray, angular housing and a wide, flat base below.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the monitor's deep, hooded casing and the flat, extended base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the smooth, unadorned back of the monitor with no visible features or ports.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the monitor's thick profile and the flat base.
attribute_screen_bezel_design: The bezel is thick, angular, and made of matte gray plastic, with sharp corners and no decorative elements or color contrast.
attribute_stand_type: The monitor is supported by a wide, flat base integrated into the housing, also matte gray, with no visible adjustability or separate stand components.
attribute_button_placement: No physical buttons or controls are visible on any side of the monitor.
